payload.answer: Brain DB is the source of truth; Cognee is a rebuildable semantic projection; Slack is a strict memory intake agent. Telegram addition and cheapest high-performance model remain open questions.
payload.decision: Record the stated architecture preferences as current context; keep the Telegram addition and model-choice items as open questions, not decisions.

entity_resolution.reason: No external entity disambiguation needed from the provided summary.

payload.intent: Extract durable memory cards from a chat summary about Brain architecture, preserving open questions as non-committal.

memory_cards.statement: Brain DB should be the source of truth.
Cognee should be a rebuildable semantic projection.
Slack should function as a strict memory intake agent.
Whether Telegram should be added later is unresolved.
The cheapest model that still saturates performance has not been determined.

details: No durable decision was inferred beyond the stated architecture preferences.

repair_options: If a later source contradicts these architecture preferences, supersede only the conflicting card and preserve the rest.
If Telegram is decided later, convert the open question into a decision card.
If a specific model is selected later, replace the model-choice open question with the chosen model and rationale.
